notes: SHARED-CLUSTER TRAP. metric-ingestor, log-ingestor and trace-ingestor are three UNRELATED consumers/producers on the SAME Kafka cluster ingest-shared but on different topics (metrics.raw, logs.raw, traces.raw); service_map.json shows NO service-call edge among them. A retention.ms config change (commit f60a4b2) applied at 14:38 made broker-2's log dir fill to 95%, dropping partitions out of the in-sync replica set CLUSTER-WIDE (UnderReplicatedPartitions 0->71). With isr < min.insync.replicas=2, produce/consume on ALL topics fails (NotEnoughReplicas / produce timeouts), so all three ingestors degrade within the same ~2-minute window. The only common link is the shared cluster, visible in service_map (kafka_cluster=ingest-shared on all three) and events.json (broker-2 log dir 95% / ISR shrink). billing (cluster billing-events + rds:billing-pg) and api-gateway are NOT on this cluster and stay healthy. auth-service deployed near onset (jwks rotation) with its own flat 401s and is off-cluster. metric-ingestor is loudest/paged but is one of three co-victims.
_design_note: Paged on metric-ingestor because it is the most customer-visible ingestor. It is one of several producers/consumers that failed at the same time on cluster ingest-shared; check WHY (cluster-level ISR shrink from broker-2 disk), not just metric-ingestor's own logs.
